title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
summary: Astrobotic will sell to Voyager Technologies to accelerate scaling for NASA's lunar base initiative. The acquisition aims to meet projected demands in lunar lander development.

title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms plans to establish a sovereign LEO satellite constellation in India by leasing broadband capacity from existing satellite networks, ahead of its IPO. This initiative aims to bolster India's independent satellite broadband infrastructure.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring smallsat manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to strengthen its position in the U.S. government market. The deal provides MDA Space with greater access to lucrative contracts in the United States.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace Inc. raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and space environment utilization platforms.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states the space industry is prepared to expand solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company emphasizes the need for predictable demand to support supplier growth.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability and argues that Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware will not meet the demands of the emerging orbital economy. It highlights the differences between various orbital regions and their respective challenges.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of the interactions between solar activity and atmospheric conditions.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will participate in international missions to study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather scientific data about the asteroid's composition and trajectory.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program, contributing to Europe's push for greater space sovereignty.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 215 — Disclosure Day
summary: Episode 215 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with sci-fi/sci-fact author Dr. David Brin on Disclosure Day. The episode is hosted by Rod Pyle and Susan Karlin.

title: Supermassive black holes may be surrounded by dark matter clusters, new 'echo map' technique suggests
summary: A new 'echo map' technique suggests that supermassive black holes, including the one at the center of the Milky Way, may be surrounded by clusters of dark matter. This discovery could provide insights into the mysterious nature of dark matter.

title: Late, great singer-songwriter Elliott Smith finds immortality as an asteroid
summary: An asteroid has been named after late singer-songwriter Elliott Smith, who passed away in 2003. This posthumous honor immortalizes him in space.

title: Do we need a lunar building code to build moon bases safely?
summary: As NASA aims to establish a long-term human presence on the moon, an expert suggests the need for a lunar building code to ensure safe construction of moon bases. This highlights the challenges and standards required for sustainable lunar infrastructure.

title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
summary: A California-based private company will design, build, and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 for the Aeolus mission, marking the first probe to provide daily global environmental measurements of Mars.

title: 'This is not like life that we’ve experienced' 'Star City' cast on struggling to relate to the brutality of Soviet existence in Apple TV's For All Mankind' spin-off
summary: Apple TV+'s For All Mankind spin-off 'Star City' explores the harsh realities of Soviet life, with cast members struggling to relate to its brutality. The show contrasts this with their own experiences, highlighting the stark differences in living conditions.

title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is losing orbit and may require a commercial spacecraft to rescue it within nine months, a task previously deemed impossible.

title: Menstruation in space will be studied for 1st time with 'Operation Period'
summary: A new study called 'Operation Period' will investigate menstruation in space for the first time. This research aims to address practical questions astronauts face, such as the '100 tampons' query.

title: Annie Easley, a hero of NASA | Space photo of the day for June 19, 2026
summary: Annie Easley, a pioneering NASA human computer, is featured in a space photo of the day for June 19, 2026. She contributed to the development of the Centaur upper-stage rocket.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has been analyzing distinct rock layers on Mount Sharp, each with unique textures and tones. The mission is part of ongoing geological surveys to understand Mars' past environmental changes.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study space weather impacts on Earth's atmosphere and improve predictions for technology like GPS and satellites. The mission advances to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has selected eight new companies and six existing contract holders to acquire commercial satellite data, expanding research and decision-making resources. The new data will supplement NASA's Earth satellites with high-resolution and frequent observations.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shares his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA. His story highlights resilience and determination in overcoming adversity.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested a prototype rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert in March 2026. The rover, designed for advanced mobility and robotic autonomy, served as a testbed for autonomy software.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing the ERNEST rover in the Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy for future Moon and Mars missions. The prototype recently traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that the asteroid Donaldjohanson is wobbly and peanut-shaped, formed from fragments after a violent collision 155 million years ago. The asteroid has undergone significant activity since its formation.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Cloud. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs in a cold gas and dust filament beyond the Orion Nebula.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright in X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. Observations revealed that the cluster is actually two clusters merging along the line of sight.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with its second stage now transported to Stennis Space Center. The company is on track for a 2026 debut of the vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed its cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is approaching a static fire test. The focus remains on post-Flight 12 developments.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has announced the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This marks a significant step toward the agency's goal of returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams are preparing Booster 20 for cryogenic testing ahead of the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The news discusses a significant advancement in robotic arm technology for space applications. This innovation is expected to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. It highlights the need for robust and precise technology in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A unique 'bake-out' process for parachutes was conducted to test their thermal performance. This test is likely related to a space mission or technology development.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite, ahead of a planned 2027 flight. The milestone marks progress in quantum communication technology for space applications.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of its Mission X-3 test objectives with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, clearing it for full commissioning.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies has raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans its first GEO mission in 2027, following a near-term mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace secured $25 million in Series B funding to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a dip in stocks of other publicly traded space companies. Major players in the industry experienced a significant pullback among investors.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking the first successful flight of its new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract. The company secured the contract from the Department of Defense.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has signed a deal with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration leverages next-generation satellite capabilities to enhance flood management in Norway.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described it as a 'defining milestone' for the firm.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation last month through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund is investing $26 million in the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite initiative. This investment supports the build-out of the constellation aimed at enhancing wildfire detection and response.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the space industry. It also mentions some breaking news related to the conversation.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the mission to prioritize work on space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy update.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research to safeguard critical infrastructure. This effort is part of the Aerospace SPARTA framework aimed at enhancing space security.
